Spiritual blessings had been promised through symbolic references to earthly abundance. Seasonable rain is a great blessing, which we may ask of God when there is most need, and we may expect it to come. We must in our prayers ask for mercies at their proper time. The Lord would make bright clouds and give showers of rain. This may be an encouragement to seek the influences of the Holy Spirit, in faith and by prayer, through which the blessings promised are obtained and enjoyed. The prophet shows the foolishness of making requests to idols, as their fathers had done. The Lord visited the remnant of his flock in mercy, and was about to renew their courage and strength for conflict and victory. Every creature is what God makes it to be. Everyone raised to support the nation, like a cornerstone supports a building, or to unite those who differ, like nails join different timbers, must come from the Lord; and those employed to overcome their enemies must have strength and success from him. This may apply to Christ; to him we must look to raise up people to unite, support, and defend his people. He never will say, Seek me in vain.

Here are precious promises to the people of God, which refer to the state of the Jews, and even to the later times of the church. Preaching the gospel is God’s call for souls to come to Jesus Christ. Those whom Christ redeemed by his blood, God will gather by his grace. Difficulties will be overcome easily and effectively, as those in the way of deliverance from Egypt. God himself will be their strength and their song. When we resist and so overcome our spiritual enemies, then our hearts shall rejoice. If God strengthens us, we must be active in all the duties of Christian life, must be active in the work of God; and we must do all in the name of the Lord Jesus.
